openFindings.title: Strict validation is claimed as complete, but the current strict validator fails
openFindings.summary: The packet requires validation checks and success criteria say packet validation passes after repair, while the checklist marks strict validator commands run. A current strict validation run exits 2 because continuity timestamps lag graph metadata; the implementation summary also records only 'Rerun after packet repair' rather than PASS.

openFindings.title: Research JSONL config still points at the pre-migration spec folder
openFindings.summary: The canonical deep-research config and graph metadata use the current 006/004 path, but the first JSONL config record still names the old 021-smart-router-context-efficacy path. Resume/replay tools that trust line 1 can re-anchor to stale lineage.

openFindings.title: description.json parentChain keeps the old phase slug after migration
openFindings.summary: description.json says the parent chain ends in 021-smart-router-context-efficacy while the canonical packet id and graph parent use 004-smart-router-context-efficacy. The migration aliases explain the old slug, but parentChain itself is stale.

openFindings.title: Verification summary leaves the strict-validator result ambiguous
openFindings.summary: The summary's verification table lists 'Rerun after packet repair' for strict spec validation instead of a pass/fail result, while checklist/tasks mark validation complete. Future maintainers have to rerun the validator to learn the actual state.

openFindings.title: decision-record.md is absent and not explicitly marked non-applicable
openFindings.summary: Level 2 packets do not require decision-record.md, but this review scope requested it and the packet does contain multiple explicit decisions. The source-doc/key-file lists omit a decision record without noting that the omission is intentional.

openFindings.title: Security/no-secret checklist evidence is assertion-only
openFindings.summary: The packet asserts no hardcoded secrets and lists deterministic validation commands, but the evidence set does not show a secret scan or equivalent command. Risk is low because artifacts are Markdown/JSON research files, but the security checklist evidence is weaker than the completed P0 marker implies.

verdict.rule: P0 -> FAIL; >=5 P1 -> CONDITIONAL; else PASS
